title: quercus × mccormickii var. koreana
intro: Quercus × mccormickii var. koreana, also known as Korean white oak, is a deciduous tree native to Korea, characterized by its white bark and broad leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus × mccormickii var. koreana include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, decoctions, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and treat various health conditions.
